results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE OVERARCHING GOAL IS TO ASSESS THE PERFORMANCE AND FEASIBILITY TRADEOFFS OF INTEGRATING MEASUREMENTS ACROSS MEASUREMENT PLATFORMS TO ASSESS EMISSIONS AND MITIGATION OF METHANE AND VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUNDS (VOCS) FROM LANDFILLS. ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT WILL: 1) ASSESS THE UNCERTAINTY IN LANDFILL METHANE EMISSIONS AT A REPRESENTATIVE SET OF THREE LANDFILLS USING A COMBINATION OF GROUND-BASED, AIRCRAFT, AND SATELLITE TOOLS; 2) DEVELOP METHANE EMISSIONS DATA FOR THREE DIFFERENT LANDFILLS FROM INTERANNUAL DOWN TO AN HOURLY TEMPORAL RESOLUTION, AND USE IT TO IMPROVE METHANE EMISSIONS MODELS; 3) DEVELOP A LANDFILL VOC EMISSIONS DATA SET THAT CAN BE USED TO ESTIMATE SPECIATED EMISSIONS BASED ON WASTE AND ENVIRONMENTAL PARAMETERS; 4) ASSESS INEXPENSIVE GROUND-BASED SYSTEMS TO ROUTINELY MONITOR VOCS; AND 5) ASSESS THE ABILITY OF VARIOUS MEASUREMENT SYSTEMS TO OBSERVE THE PERFORMANCE OF REAL-WORLD EMISSIONS MITIGATIONS. SUBRECIPIENT:THE SUBAWARD TO FORT LEWIS WILL BE FOR $50,000. THEY WILL BE INVOLVED IN THE DESIGN, FABRICATION, AND APPLICATION OF DYNAMIC FLUX CHAMBERS FOR USE IN MEASUREMENT LANDFILL GAS FLUXES.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL RESEARCH REPORTS. OTHER EXPECTED DELIVERABLES INCLUDE DATASETS OF TEMPORALLY AND SPATIALLY RESOLVED METHANE AND VOC SPECIES CONCENTRATIONS SPANNING 0.5-1.5 YEARS FOR THREE LANDFILLS AS WELL DATASETS OF METHANE AND VOC SPECIES EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE ADVANCEMENTS IN APPROACHES OF QUANTIFYING LANDFILL EMISSIONS AND IMPROVED UNDERSTANDING OF FACTORS THAT AFFECT EMISSIONS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE RESIDENTS AFFECTED BY HAZARDOUS AIR POLLUTANTS EMITTED FROM LANDFILLS TO BE STUDIES AS WELL THE GENERAL PUBLIC, WHO ARE AFFECTED BY METHANE'S CONTRIBUTION TO CLIMATE CHANGE.
